what_matters_in_this_vertical: Visible social proof and review integration at point of conversion
Local search visibility for dining and food queries in Westport
Content freshness to capture seasonal and tourism-driven traffic
Mobile experience quality for on-the-go visitors
likely_customer_intent: Find a Thai restaurant in Westport and check it looks reputable
Read reviews before deciding where to eat
Make a booking or check availability quickly
Browse the menu to confirm the food suits them
Get directions or contact details while already in the area
commercial_risk_if_unaddressed: Without fresh content and local schema in place, the site may underperform on location-based search queries that drive tourist and visitor traffic in a seasonal destination like Westport. As competitor restaurants improve their online presence, a technically strong site that lacks visible social proof and indexable content updates risks losing booking intent to businesses that appear more active and credible in local search results.
strongest_positioning_angle: a Thai Noodle Bar already has an excellent technical foundation and strong review reputation, making it well-placed to convert more of its existing traffic with targeted trust-surfacing and local content that require minimal lift to implement.
top_3_outreach_talking_points: Your site is technically one of the best-performing restaurant sites we have reviewed in the region, and there are a handful of small additions that could make it work even harder for you during the busy tourist season.

You have 102 Google reviews averaging 4.6 stars, which is a genuinely strong asset. Bringing even a few of those reviews onto the site itself, near the booking button, gives first-time visitors the confidence to book without leaving the page.

Westport attracts significant visitor traffic and there is a clear opportunity to capture more of those searches with a light content section covering seasonal menus or local dining guides, without requiring a major time commitment from you.
likely_objections: The site is already doing well, so we are not sure what needs to change
We get a lot of bookings through word of mouth and do not rely heavily on the website
We do not have time to write blog posts or update content regularly
We already have someone who built the site and handles it if something breaks
recommended_offer_angle: Position an entry-level managed maintenance and content support package that handles WordPress updates, backups, and uptime monitoring, with a monthly light-touch content or menu update included. Frame this as protecting and incrementally building on the performance already achieved rather than fixing anything broken.
unknowns_to_clarify: Are Google reviews currently featured anywhere on the website, or only visible externally on Google Maps?
Which third-party booking system is in use, and has the mobile booking flow been tested for completion rate and load reliability?
Does the business have active social media profiles, and are these linked from the website?
Is there currently any maintenance or hosting support arrangement in place, or is the site largely self-managed since launch?
